sentence_reviews.text: Random variables, distributions, conditional dependence, and expectation provide the language of uncertainty and generative modeling.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA newchap20.pdf, Section 20.1 'Statistical Learning' (lines 19-21, 34-37, 42-55, 59-63); Probability theory - Wikipedia, introductory definition and 'Treatment' / 'Probability theory' sections (lines 259-263, 283-287); Generative model - Wikipedia, 'Definition' (lines 152-159, 171-174).
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited sources directly support random variables, distributions, conditional probability/independence, expectation, and generative modeling as probabilistic concepts. No single source states the full sentence verbatim, so this is an atlas synthesis rather than direct verification.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Probability theory is to connect lower-level principles to downstream capabilities including Statistics & experimental design, Information theory, Signal processing & harmonic analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probability theory - Wikipedia, lines 259-263; AIMA newchap20.pdf, lines 147-163; Stochastic process - Wikipedia, lines 283-284.
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources directly support probability as a foundation for statistics, and they support links to information-theoretic reasoning and signal-processing applications through stochastic processes. The exact 'connect lower-level principles to downstream capabilities' wording is editorial synthesis.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probability theory - Wikipedia, line 263 ('As a mathematical foundation for statistics...'); AIMA newchap20.pdf, lines 157-163 (maximum-likelihood learning common in statistics).
sentence_reviews.notes: The foundation-for-statistics relation is directly supported; 'substrate for' and the experimental-design extension are atlas phrasing, not source wording.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA newchap20.pdf, lines 147-156 (connection between probability, information encoding, and compression); Stochastic process - Wikipedia, line 284 (applications include information theory).
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited text supports the probability/information-theoretic link, but the exact 'substrate for' wording is a synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Signal processing & harmonic analysis
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Stochastic process - Wikipedia, lines 283-284 (applications include signal processing); Probability theory - Wikipedia, lines 261-263 (random variables, stochastic processes, and quantitative analysis).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support signal-processing applications of stochastic processes, but they do not directly support harmonic analysis or the stronger 'substrate for' phrasing. Narrowing would make this safer.

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA newchap20.pdf, lines 61-63, 115-118, 130-137, 157-163; Probability theory - Wikipedia, lines 259-263.
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA explicitly discusses i.i.d. assumptions, priors, approximation, and large-data caveats; the empirical-mismatch warning is a reasonable synthesis but not quoted verbatim.
